name: NCSC Cyber Assessment Framework (CAF) v4.0 Ontology
description: Comprehensive ontology representing the UK National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) Cyber Assessment Framework v4.0, enabling AI agents to reason about cyber security governance, risk management, protection controls, detection capabilities, and incident response across UK public sector and critical national infrastructure organisations. Models the full CAF hierarchy of 4 Objectives, 14 Principles, 41 Contributing Outcomes, and ~559 Indicators of Good Practice (IGPs) with three-tier achievement assessment (Not Achieved, Partially Achieved, Achieved). Supports CAF Profile customisation for sector-specific target outcomes as mandated by NIS Regulations and the UK Government Cyber Security Strategy.
oaa:domain: UK Public Sector Cyber Security & Compliance

oaa:sourceFramework.name: NCSC Cyber Assessment Framework
applicability: Operators of Essential Services (OES) under NIS Regulations 2018
UK Local Government (CAF for Local Government profile)
NHS Trusts and health sector organisations
Organisations seeking alignment with UK cyber resilience expectations

oaa:imports.name: GRC-FW-ONT
oaa:imports.description: RCSG Framework providing ComplianceFramework entity for CAF certification tracking and GRCContext for organisational compliance posture

oaa:imports.name: MCSB-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark providing SecurityControl for cross-framework control mapping between CAF and Azure/cloud security controls

oaa:imports.name: RMF-IS27005-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Risk Management Framework providing Risk and Control entities for CAF Objective A risk management alignment

oaa:imports.name: DSPT-ONT
oaa:imports.description: NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit providing DSPTStandard and DSPTAssertion entities for health sector compliance alignment — DSPT standards map to CAF contributing outcomes for NHS organisations

oaa:joinPatterns.name: CAF Compliance Framework Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: CAF assessments link to GRC-FW ComplianceFramework for organisational compliance posture tracking
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Track CAF assessment status within organisational RCSG compliance landscape

oaa:joinPatterns.name: CAF to MCSB Control Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: CAF Contributing Outcomes in Objective B map to MCSB SecurityControls for cloud-specific implementation guidance
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Map CAF protection outcomes to Azure/cloud security benchmark controls

oaa:joinPatterns.name: CAF Risk Management Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: CAF Objective A risk management outcomes align to RMF risk assessment and treatment entities
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Align CAF risk governance expectations with ISO 27005 risk management practices

oaa:joinPatterns.name: CAF RCSG Context Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: CAF assessments operate within the organisation's overarching RCSG governance context for holistic compliance posture tracking
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Embed CAF assessment within organisational risk, compliance, security, and governance posture

oaa:joinPatterns.name: CAF to RMF Risk Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: CAF outcome assessment gaps trace to identified information security risks in the RMF risk register for risk-based prioritisation
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Link CAF assessment gaps to identified risks for risk-based remediation prioritisation

entities.name: CAFObjective
entities.description: One of the four top-level objectives of the NCSC Cyber Assessment Framework v4.0. Objectives define the overarching security outcomes an organisation must achieve: (A) Managing Security Risk, (B) Protecting Against Cyber Attack, (C) Detecting Cyber Security Events, and (D) Minimising the Impact of Cyber Security Incidents.

oaa:properties.name: objectiveCode
oaa:properties.description: Single-letter objective identifier (A, B, C, or D)

oaa:properties.name: objectiveName
oaa:properties.description: Full name of the objective

oaa:properties.name: objectiveDescription
oaa:properties.description: Detailed description of what the objective covers and why it matters for organisational cyber resilience

oaa:properties.name: principleCount
oaa:properties.description: Number of principles under this objective

oaa:instances.objectiveDescription: Appropriate organisational structures, policies, and processes are in place to understand, assess, and systematically manage security risks to the network and information systems supporting essential functions.

Proportionate security measures are in place to protect essential functions and systems from cyber attack, including identity management, data protection, system hardening, resilience, and staff training.
Security monitoring and proactive threat hunting capabilities ensure that cyber security events affecting essential functions are detected in a timely manner.

oaa:instances.objectiveName: Minimising the Impact of Cyber Security Incidents
oaa:instances.objectiveDescription: Capabilities exist to minimise the adverse impact of a cyber security incident on the operation of essential functions, including response planning, recovery, and lessons learned.

entities.name: CAFPrinciple
entities.description: One of 14 principles within the CAF that group related contributing outcomes under an objective. Each principle represents a distinct area of cyber security practice that organisations must address.

oaa:properties.name: principleCode
oaa:properties.description: Principle identifier in format ObjectiveLetter + Number (e.g., A1, B2, C1, D2)

oaa:properties.name: principleName
oaa:properties.description: Full name of the principle

oaa:properties.name: principleDescription
oaa:properties.description: Description of the security practice area this principle covers

oaa:properties.name: contributingOutcomeCount
oaa:properties.description: Number of contributing outcomes under this principle

oaa:instances.principleDescription: The organisation has appropriate management policies and processes in place to govern its approach to the security of network and information systems.
The organisation takes appropriate steps to identify, assess, and understand security risks and to select appropriate and proportionate security measures.
Everything required to deliver, maintain, or support networks and information systems for essential functions is determined, understood, and managed.
The organisation understands and manages security risks to network and information systems arising from the supply chain, including secure software development and support.

oaa:instances.principleName: Service Protection Policies, Processes and Procedures
oaa:instances.principleDescription: The organisation defines, implements, communicates, and enforces appropriate policies, processes, and procedures to protect essential functions.
The organisation understands, documents, and manages access to network and information systems supporting essential functions.
Data stored on or transmitted across network and information systems supporting essential functions is protected from actions that may cause an adverse impact.
Network and information systems supporting essential functions are protected from cyber attack through secure design, configuration, management, and vulnerability handling.
The organisation builds resilience against cyber attack into the design, implementation, operation, and management of network and information systems.
Staff are appropriately supported with cyber security awareness, training, and exercising, enabling a security-positive culture across the organisation.
The organisation monitors the security status of network and information systems in order to detect potential security problems and track the effectiveness of existing security measures.
The organisation proactively searches for indicators of compromise and actively hunts for threats that may have bypassed existing detection mechanisms.
The organisation maintains an effective incident response plan and capability, with defined processes, roles, and tested recovery procedures.
When cyber security incidents occur, lessons are identified and acted upon to improve the security of network and information systems.

entities.name: ContributingOutcome
entities.description: A specific measurable outcome within a CAF Principle, assessed against Indicators of Good Practice (IGPs) at three achievement levels. There are 41 Contributing Outcomes across the 14 principles in CAF v4.0. Each outcome represents a concrete security capability or practice that an organisation must demonstrate.

oaa:properties.name: outcomeCode
oaa:properties.description: Contributing Outcome identifier in format PrincipleCode.letter (e.g., A1.a, B2.c, D1.b)

oaa:properties.name: outcomeName
oaa:properties.description: Short name describing the outcome area

oaa:properties.name: outcomeDescription
oaa:properties.description: Detailed description of the expected security outcome

oaa:properties.name: igpCountNotAchieved
oaa:properties.description: Number of IGPs at Not Achieved level — any single 'true' means outcome is Not Achieved

oaa:properties.name: igpCountPartiallyAchieved
oaa:properties.description: Number of IGPs at Partially Achieved level — ALL must be 'true' with no Not Achieved indicators

oaa:properties.name: igpCountAchieved
oaa:properties.description: Number of IGPs at Achieved level — ALL must be 'true' with no Not Achieved indicators

oaa:properties.name: isNewInV4
oaa:properties.description: Whether this contributing outcome is new or significantly revised in CAF v4.0

oaa:instances.outcomeName: Sources and Tools for Logging and Monitoring
Personnel Skills for Monitoring and Detection
Understanding Users and Systems Behaviour, and Threat Intelligence

entities.name: IndicatorOfGoodPractice
entities.description: An individual assessment statement (IGP) within a Contributing Outcome at a specific achievement level. IGPs provide the concrete criteria against which organisations are assessed. CAF v4.0 contains approximately 559 IGPs across all contributing outcomes. Assessment logic: if ANY 'Not Achieved' IGP is true, the outcome is Not Achieved. To reach 'Partially Achieved' or 'Achieved', ALL IGPs at that level must be true with no 'Not Achieved' indicators being true.

oaa:properties.name: igpId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for the IGP within its contributing outcome and achievement level

oaa:properties.name: igpText
oaa:properties.description: The full text of the indicator statement describing good or poor practice

oaa:properties.name: achievementLevel
oaa:properties.description: The achievement level this IGP belongs to: NotAchieved, PartiallyAchieved, or Achieved

oaa:properties.name: isNegativeIndicator
oaa:properties.description: True for 'Not Achieved' IGPs which describe poor practice — if any are true, the outcome fails

entities.name: CAFAssessment
entities.description: A structured assessment exercise evaluating an organisation against the CAF framework, producing outcome-level results across all or a subset of contributing outcomes. Assessments may be self-assessments, peer reviews, regulator-led, or conducted by third-party assessors under GovAssure or NIS competent authority programmes.

oaa:properties.name: assessmentId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for this assessment exercise

oaa:properties.name: organisationName
oaa:properties.description: Name of the organisation being assessed

oaa:properties.name: assessmentDate
oaa:properties.description: Date the assessment was conducted or completed

oaa:properties.name: assessmentType
oaa:properties.description: Type of assessment conducted

oaa:properties.name: assessor
oaa:properties.description: Individual or organisation conducting the assessment

oaa:properties.name: cafVersion
oaa:properties.description: Version of the CAF framework used (e.g., 4.0)

oaa:properties.name: profileApplied
oaa:properties.description: CAF Profile applied if sector-specific (e.g., Local Government, CNI-Energy)

oaa:properties.name: scope
oaa:properties.description: Essential function(s) or systems in scope for this assessment

oaa:properties.name: overallComplianceScore
oaa:properties.description: Percentage of contributing outcomes assessed as Achieved or Partially Achieved (0.0-100.0)

oaa:properties.name: outcomesAchieved
oaa:properties.description: Count of contributing outcomes assessed as Achieved

oaa:properties.name: outcomesPartiallyAchieved
oaa:properties.description: Count of contributing outcomes assessed as Partially Achieved

oaa:properties.name: outcomesNotAchieved
oaa:properties.description: Count of contributing outcomes assessed as Not Achieved

oaa:properties.name: status
oaa:properties.description: Current status of the assessment

oaa:properties.name: nextReviewDate
oaa:properties.description: Recommended date for reassessment

entities.name: OutcomeAssessment
entities.description: The assessment result for a single Contributing Outcome within a CAF Assessment, recording the achievement level, evidence, gaps, and remediation actions. This is the primary audit artefact for compliance verification.

oaa:properties.name: outcomeCode
oaa:properties.description: The Contributing Outcome code being assessed (e.g., A1.a, B4.d)

oaa:properties.name: achievementLevel
oaa:properties.description: Assessed achievement level for this outcome

oaa:properties.name: igpsNotAchievedTrue
oaa:properties.description: Number of 'Not Achieved' IGPs found to be true (any > 0 means outcome fails)

oaa:properties.name: igpsPartiallyAchievedTrue
oaa:properties.description: Number of 'Partially Achieved' IGPs confirmed true

oaa:properties.name: igpsAchievedTrue
oaa:properties.description: Number of 'Achieved' IGPs confirmed true

oaa:properties.name: evidenceSummary
oaa:properties.description: Summary of evidence supporting the assessment determination

oaa:properties.name: gaps
oaa:properties.description: Identified gaps preventing a higher achievement level

oaa:properties.name: remediationPlan
oaa:properties.description: Planned actions to address gaps and improve achievement level

oaa:properties.name: remediationPriority
oaa:properties.description: Priority level for remediation if gaps exist

oaa:properties.name: remediationOwner
oaa:properties.description: Individual or team accountable for remediation delivery

oaa:properties.name: targetAchievementLevel
oaa:properties.description: Target achievement level set by the applicable CAF Profile

oaa:properties.name: targetDate
oaa:properties.description: Target date for achieving the target achievement level

entities.name: CAFProfile
entities.description: A sector-specific or regulator-defined target set of achievement levels for Contributing Outcomes. CAF Profiles allow competent authorities to set minimum acceptable outcomes for their regulated sector. Examples include the GovAssure profile for central government, the Local Government CAF Profile, and sector-specific CNI profiles for energy, water, transport, and health.

oaa:properties.name: profileId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for this CAF Profile

oaa:properties.name: profileName
oaa:properties.description: Name of the CAF Profile (e.g., GovAssure 2025, Local Government Baseline)

oaa:properties.name: sectorApplicability
oaa:properties.description: Sector(s) this profile applies to (e.g., Central Government, Local Government, NHS, Energy, Water)

oaa:properties.name: competentAuthority
oaa:properties.description: Regulator or competent authority that defined this profile

oaa:properties.name: minimumAchievementDefault
oaa:properties.description: Default minimum achievement level expected for all outcomes unless overridden

oaa:properties.name: profileVersion
oaa:properties.description: Version of the profile

oaa:properties.name: cafVersion
oaa:properties.description: CAF version this profile is aligned to

oaa:properties.name: effectiveDate
oaa:properties.description: Date from which this profile takes effect

entities.name: ProfileOutcomeTarget
entities.description: A specific target achievement level within a CAF Profile for a particular Contributing Outcome, overriding the profile default where sector-specific requirements demand a higher or different standard.

oaa:properties.name: outcomeCode
oaa:properties.description: The Contributing Outcome code targeted

oaa:properties.name: targetAchievementLevel
oaa:properties.description: Required achievement level for this outcome in this profile

oaa:properties.name: justification
oaa:properties.description: Reason for setting this specific target (e.g., regulatory mandate, threat landscape)

entities.name: ComplianceMapping
entities.description: Cross-framework mapping linking a CAF Contributing Outcome to controls, requirements, or clauses in other compliance frameworks (ISO 27001, NIST CSF, Cyber Essentials, MCSB, GDPR). Enables multi-framework audit evidence reuse and gap analysis.

oaa:properties.name: mappingId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for this mapping

oaa:properties.name: targetFramework
oaa:properties.description: Name of the target compliance framework

oaa:properties.name: targetControlId
oaa:properties.description: Control or clause identifier in the target framework

oaa:properties.name: targetControlName
oaa:properties.description: Name or title of the control in the target framework

oaa:properties.name: mappingType
oaa:properties.description: Strength of the mapping relationship

oaa:properties.name: mappingNotes
oaa:properties.description: Additional context about how the frameworks relate

entities.name: ComplianceFramework
entities.description: External GRC-FW entity representing an organisational compliance certification tracked within the RCSG context.

entities.name: GRCContext
entities.description: External GRC-FW entity representing the organisation's overarching risk, compliance, security, and governance posture.

entities.name: SecurityControl
entities.description: External MCSB entity representing an individual cloud security benchmark control for cross-framework control mapping.

entities.name: ControlDomain
entities.description: External MCSB entity representing a high-level security control domain for aggregate mapping.

entities.name: Risk
entities.description: External RMF-IS27005 entity representing an identified information security risk for risk-outcome traceability.

entities.name: Control
entities.description: External RMF-IS27005 entity representing an ISO 27001 Annex A security control for risk treatment alignment.

entities.name: RiskAssessment
entities.description: External RMF-IS27005 entity representing a structured risk assessment exercise for CAF Objective A alignment.

oaa:enumerations.name: Achievement Level
oaa:enumerations.description: Three-tier assessment scale for CAF Contributing Outcomes. Not Achieved indicates poor practice is evident. Partially Achieved indicates basic security measures are in place against common threats. Achieved indicates comprehensive security measures are in place against capable and well-resourced threat actors.

values.name: Not Achieved
values.description: Poor practice is evident — one or more 'Not Achieved' IGPs are true, indicating fundamental security deficiencies that must be addressed urgently

values.name: Partially Achieved
values.description: Basic security measures are in place against common threats — all 'Partially Achieved' IGPs are true and no 'Not Achieved' indicators are present

values.name: Achieved
values.description: Comprehensive security measures are in place proportionate to capable and well-resourced threat actors — all 'Achieved' IGPs are true and no 'Not Achieved' indicators are present

oaa:enumerations.name: Assessment Type
oaa:enumerations.description: Classification of CAF assessment exercises by conducting authority

values.name: Self-Assessment
values.description: Organisation-led assessment for internal assurance and improvement

values.name: Peer Review
values.description: Assessment conducted by peer organisation or sector group

values.name: Third-Party Assessment
values.description: Independent assessment by NCSC-assured or qualified third party

values.name: Regulator-Led Assessment
values.description: Assessment conducted by or on behalf of a NIS competent authority

values.name: GovAssure Assessment
values.description: Central government assessment under the GovAssure programme

oaa:enumerations.name: Assessment Status
oaa:enumerations.description: Lifecycle status of a CAF assessment

values.name: Planned
values.description: Assessment scheduled but not yet started

values.name: In Progress
values.description: Assessment underway, evidence being gathered

values.name: Completed
values.description: Assessment complete, results finalised

values.name: Remediation
values.description: Assessment complete, remediation actions underway

values.name: Closed
values.description: Assessment cycle complete, all actions resolved or accepted

oaa:enumerations.name: Remediation Priority
oaa:enumerations.description: Priority levels for addressing gaps identified in CAF assessment

values.name: Critical
values.description: Not Achieved outcome in a profile-mandated area — immediate action required

values.name: High
values.description: Not Achieved outcome or significant gap in Achieved target — action within 30 days

values.name: Medium
values.description: Partially Achieved where Achieved is targeted — action within 90 days

values.name: Low
values.description: Improvement opportunity, not a compliance gap — action within next assessment cycle

oaa:enumerations.name: Mapping Type
oaa:enumerations.description: Strength of cross-framework control mapping relationship

values.name: Full
values.description: The CAF outcome and target control are directly equivalent in scope and intent

values.name: Partial
values.description: The CAF outcome covers a subset of the target control or vice versa

values.name: Related
values.description: The CAF outcome and target control address similar but not identical security concerns

relationships.name: hasPrinciple
relationships.description: A CAFObjective contains one or more CAFPrinciples that define specific security practice areas

relationships.name: belongsToObjective
relationships.description: A CAFPrinciple belongs to exactly one CAFObjective

relationships.name: hasContributingOutcome
relationships.description: A CAFPrinciple contains one or more ContributingOutcomes that define measurable security capabilities

relationships.name: belongsToPrinciple
relationships.description: A ContributingOutcome belongs to exactly one CAFPrinciple

relationships.name: hasIGP
relationships.description: A ContributingOutcome is assessed via one or more Indicators of Good Practice across achievement levels

relationships.name: assessesOutcome
relationships.description: An OutcomeAssessment evaluates a specific ContributingOutcome within a CAFAssessment

relationships.name: hasOutcomeAssessment
relationships.description: A CAFAssessment contains one or more OutcomeAssessments covering the contributing outcomes in scope

relationships.name: appliesProfile
relationships.description: A CAFAssessment applies a specific CAFProfile defining the target achievement levels for the assessed sector

relationships.name: hasOutcomeTarget
relationships.description: A CAFProfile defines specific ProfileOutcomeTargets for contributing outcomes that override the profile default

relationships.name: hasComplianceMapping
relationships.description: A ContributingOutcome has cross-framework ComplianceMappings to other security standards and frameworks

relationships.name: assessedUnderFramework
relationships.description: Links a CAFAssessment to the GRC-FW ComplianceFramework entity for organisational compliance posture tracking

relationships.name: mapsToSecurityControl
relationships.description: A ContributingOutcome maps to one or more MCSB SecurityControls for Azure/cloud-specific implementation alignment

relationships.name: mapsToControlDomain
relationships.description: A CAFPrinciple maps to one or more MCSB ControlDomains at the aggregate level

relationships.name: alignsToRiskFramework
relationships.description: CAF Objective A outcomes align to RMF-IS27005 RiskAssessment entities for ISO 27005 risk management practices

relationships.name: treatedByControl
relationships.description: A ContributingOutcome gap may be treated by implementing RMF-IS27005 Controls for risk mitigation

relationships.name: operatesWithinContext
relationships.description: A CAFAssessment operates within the organisation's overarching RCSG governance context for compliance posture alignment

relationships.name: mitigatesRisk
relationships.description: An OutcomeAssessment gap or remediation action addresses an identified information security risk from the RMF risk register

businessRules.name: Not Achieved Overrides All
businessRules.condition: IF any IndicatorOfGoodPractice with achievementLevel='NotAchieved' is assessed as TRUE for a ContributingOutcome
businessRules.action: THEN the OutcomeAssessment.achievementLevel MUST be 'Not Achieved' regardless of other IGP results

businessRules.name: Partially Achieved Requires All Partial IGPs
businessRules.action: THEN ALL IndicatorOfGoodPractice at PartiallyAchieved level MUST be TRUE AND zero NotAchieved IGPs are TRUE

businessRules.name: Achieved Requires All Achieved IGPs
businessRules.action: THEN ALL IndicatorOfGoodPractice at Achieved level MUST be TRUE AND zero NotAchieved IGPs are TRUE

businessRules.name: Assessment Must Cover All Profiled Outcomes
businessRules.action: THEN OutcomeAssessments MUST exist for ALL ContributingOutcomes referenced in the applied CAFProfile

businessRules.name: Not Achieved Requires Remediation Plan
businessRules.action: THEN OutcomeAssessment.remediationPlan MUST NOT be NULL AND OutcomeAssessment.remediationPriority MUST be 'Critical' or 'High'

businessRules.name: Below Profile Target Requires Remediation
businessRules.condition: IF OutcomeAssessment.achievementLevel < ProfileOutcomeTarget.targetAchievementLevel for the same outcomeCode
businessRules.action: THEN OutcomeAssessment.remediationPlan MUST NOT be NULL AND OutcomeAssessment.targetDate MUST be set

businessRules.name: Assessment Must Have Evidence
businessRules.action: THEN OutcomeAssessment.evidenceSummary MUST NOT be NULL or empty

businessRules.name: Completed Assessment Must Have All Outcomes
businessRules.action: THEN COUNT(OutcomeAssessment) MUST equal the total contributing outcomes in scope (41 for full CAF, or profile-specified count)

businessRules.name: GovAssure Assessment Requires Third Party
businessRules.action: THEN CAFAssessment.assessor MUST reference an NCSC-assured assessor organisation

businessRules.name: Reassessment Cadence
businessRules.action: THEN CAFAssessment.nextReviewDate SHOULD be within 12 months of assessmentDate for NIS-regulated entities

businessRules.name: UK Public Sector Minimum Baseline
businessRules.condition: IF CAFAssessment.profileApplied references a UK public sector CAF Profile
businessRules.action: THEN ALL Objective A and Objective D outcomes SHOULD achieve at minimum 'Partially Achieved' to meet baseline governance and incident response expectations

businessRules.name: Cross-Framework Mapping Completeness
businessRules.condition: IF organisation holds ISO 27001 certification or MCSB compliance
businessRules.action: THEN ComplianceMappings SHOULD exist linking relevant ContributingOutcomes to ISO 27001 controls or MCSB SecurityControls to enable audit evidence reuse

crossOntologyReferences.description: NCSC-CAF-ONT is the UK public sector compliance overlay within the RCSG-Series, bridging the NCSC Cyber Assessment Framework to GRC-FW for organisational compliance posture, MCSB for Azure/cloud security control mapping, and RMF-IS27005 for risk management alignment. CAF assessments register as ComplianceFramework entries in GRC-FW. Objective B outcomes map to MCSB security control domains for cloud-specific implementation. Objective A outcomes align to RMF risk assessment and treatment practices.

lineageAlignment.grc-fw:ComplianceFramework: GRC-FW ComplianceFramework tracks CAF assessment certification status and expiry within organisational compliance landscape
lineageAlignment.grc-fw:GRCContext: GRC-FW GRCContext provides the overarching governance context in which CAF assessments operate
lineageAlignment.mcsb:SecurityControl: MCSB SecurityControl provides Azure/cloud-specific implementation controls mapped to CAF Objective B outcomes
lineageAlignment.mcsb:ControlDomain: MCSB ControlDomain provides aggregate domain-level mapping to CAF Principles
lineageAlignment.rmf:Risk: RMF Risk represents identified information security risks that CAF outcome assessment gaps mitigate or address
lineageAlignment.rmf:RiskAssessment: RMF RiskAssessment aligns to CAF Objective A risk management contributing outcomes
lineageAlignment.rmf:Control: RMF Control provides ISO 27001 Annex A controls for CAF gap remediation treatment

ukPublicSectorFrameworkMappings.description: Key UK public sector frameworks that CAF outcomes map to for multi-framework compliance

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022
frameworks.relevance: Information security management system — CAF outcomes map to Annex A controls across all 4 objectives

frameworks.name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) v2.0
frameworks.relevance: US framework widely used in UK — Identify/Protect/Detect/Respond/Recover functions directly align to CAF objectives A-D

frameworks.name: Cyber Essentials / Cyber Essentials Plus
frameworks.relevance: UK baseline certification scheme — CAF Objective B outcomes subsume Cyber Essentials requirements (firewalls, secure config, access control, malware, patching)

frameworks.name: NCSC 10 Steps to Cyber Security
frameworks.relevance: NCSC foundational guidance — maps to CAF principles as a simplified view of the same security areas

frameworks.name: NIS Regulations 2018 (UK)
frameworks.relevance: Legal basis for CAF — NIS competent authorities use CAF to assess Operators of Essential Services

frameworks.name: GovAssure
frameworks.relevance: Central government assurance programme using CAF as the assessment framework

frameworks.name: Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark (MCSB)
frameworks.relevance: Azure-specific controls — CAF Objective B maps directly to MCSB control domains for cloud-deployed essential services

frameworks.name: GDPR / UK GDPR / Data Protection Act 2018
frameworks.relevance: Data protection regulation — CAF B3 (Data Security) and A4 (Supply Chain) outcomes align with GDPR obligations
